marketFocus: US-Iran tensions escalate sharply, military strike possible this weekend
FOMC Minutes show divided Fed outlook - some favor cuts, others prefer two-sided guidance
Strong US data (Durable Goods, Housing, Industrial Production) supports USD
RBNZ signals easing cycle likely over, maintaining accommodative policy for now
Oil rallies 4%+ on geopolitical premium, gold approaches USD 5,000/oz

USD.name: USD
USD.summary: USD strengthens on strong US data, rising yields, and safe-haven demand amid escalating US-Iran tensions. FOMC Minutes show divided outlook with several favoring two-sided guidance.
drivers: Strong US data: Durable Goods -1.4% (exp -2%), Industrial Production +0.7% (exp +0.4%)
US yields rise on better-than-expected data and geopolitical risk premium
US-Iran tensions escalate: Trump admin closer to major war, military ready to strike this weekend
FOMC Minutes: Several favor two-sided guidance, upward adjustments possible if inflation persists
Safe-haven demand as oil rallies 4%+ on Iran war fears

EUR.name: EUR
EUR.summary: EUR weakens amid USD strength and conflicting reports about ECB President Lagarde's future. Spain enters race to replace Lagarde with de Cos as strong contender.
drivers: EUR/USD slips beneath 1.1800 level on firmer dollar
Conflicting reports about ECB President Lagarde potentially stepping down before October 2027
Spain enters race to replace Lagarde, de Cos widely regarded as strong contender
ECB's Schnabel: Euro increasingly acting as haven, boosting global role
Geopolitical uncertainty weighs on risk sentiment

GBP.name: GBP
GBP.summary: GBP languishes at month's lows beneath 1.3500 after UK CPI slowed to 3.0% y/y. BoE March cut pricing rises to 95% as inflation data confirms dovish path.
drivers: UK CPI slowed to 3.0% y/y (in line), Core 3.1% (in line), Services 4.4% (exp 4.3%)
GBP/USD trades beneath 1.3500 handle on USD strength and softer inflation
BoE March cut probability rises to 95% from 84% pre-release
ING sticks with call for March cut and another by June
Dovish UK jobs report earlier in week reinforces easing bias

JPY.name: JPY
JPY.summary: JPY weakens as USD/JPY reclaims 155.00 level on higher US yields and strong US data. Japanese Machinery Orders surge but fail to support JPY amid broader USD strength.
drivers: USD/JPY rises to 155.00+ from 153.07 trough on USD strength and higher US yields
Japanese Machinery Orders surge: M/M +19.1% (exp +4.5%), Y/Y +16.8% (exp +3.9%)
10yr JGB yields fall after weaker demand at 20yr JGB auction
BoJ March hike odds remain at 60% but JPY fails to benefit from strong data
Safe-haven flows bypass JPY in favor of USD and gold

AUD.name: AUD
AUD.summary: AUD mildly benefits from positive risk appetite and mixed jobs data. ASX 200 hits fresh record high on telecoms and energy strength, but USD strength caps gains.
drivers: Australian Employment Change +17.8K (exp +20K), Unemployment Rate 4.1% (exp 4.2%)
Full-time employment rose +50.5K, offsetting part-time weakness
ASX 200 hits fresh record high on telecoms (Telstra +9.3% H1 profit) and energy strength
AUD/USD supported following jobs data but capped by firmer USD
RBA Minutes due Monday, Wage Price Index Tuesday - key catalysts ahead

CAD.name: CAD
CAD.summary: CAD trades mixed as oil rally provides support but USD strength weighs. Canadian minister signs 15 commercial partnerships in Mexico, reassured by USMCA trilateral commitment.
drivers: Oil rallies 4%+ on US-Iran tensions, WTI settles at USD 65.05/bbl (+USD 2.79)
Canadian minister signs 15 commercial partnerships in Mexico
Mexico reassures Canada of desire to work together on USMCA trilateral agreement
LeBlanc to meet with USTR Greer in coming weeks to discuss CUSMA
USD strength offsets oil-related support for CAD

CHF.name: CHF
CHF.summary: CHF trades rangebound as safe-haven demand is split between USD and gold. SNB policy remains accommodative with rates at 0.00%.
drivers: CHF fails to benefit from safe-haven flows as USD and gold dominate
SNB policy remains accommodative with rates at 0.00%
Swiss franc range-bound amid mixed risk sentiment
Geopolitical tensions favor USD over CHF as primary safe-haven
No major Swiss data this week to drive CHF direction

NZD.name: NZD
NZD.summary: NZD clear underperformer after RBNZ holds OCR at 2.25% with dovish commentary. RBNZ signals easing cycle likely over but maintains accommodative policy for now.
drivers: RBNZ holds OCR at 2.25% as expected, signals easing cycle likely over
RBNZ Governor Breman: Not comfortable with inflation at 3.1%, fundamentals consistent with slower inflation
RBNZ Assistant Governor Silk: Maintaining accommodative policy for a while aligns with economic conditions
NZD/USD dips under 0.60, trades at 0.5978-0.6053 range
Dovish skew in RBNZ commentary weighs on NZD despite neutral policy stance

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: UST yields rise on stronger-than-expected US data, rising energy prices, and weak 20yr auction. FOMC Minutes show divided outlook with several favoring two-sided guidance.
drivers: 10yr UST futures languish at week's lows after retreating on strong US data
Rising energy prices (oil +4%) add inflationary pressure
Weak 20yr auction (high yield 4.664%, tails 2bps) weighs on sentiment
FOMC Minutes: Several favor two-sided guidance, upward adjustments possible if inflation persists

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bund yields rise on ECB President Lagarde succession uncertainty and geopolitical tensions. Spain enters race to replace Lagarde with de Cos as strong contender.
drivers: Bunds spend day on backfoot within 129.15-129.39 range
FT reports ECB President Lagarde could step down before October 2027 term end
Spain enters race to replace Lagarde, de Cos widely regarded as strong contender
Geopolitical uncertainty weighs on risk sentiment

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: JGB yields fall despite strong Machinery Orders data as weaker demand at 20yr JGB auction weighs. BoJ March hike odds remain at 60% but bond market shows caution.
drivers: 10yr JGB futures retreat after weaker demand at 20yr JGB auction
Japanese Machinery Orders surge: M/M +19.1% (exp +4.5%), Y/Y +16.8% (exp +3.9%)
BoJ March hike odds remain at 60% but bond market shows caution
Safe-haven demand supports JGBs amid geopolitical tensions

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilt yields fall after UK CPI data confirms dovish BoE path. March cut probability rises to 95% as inflation slows to 3.0% y/y, reinforcing easing bias.
drivers: Gilts lower by handful of ticks, trade at midpoint of 92.03-92.32 range
UK CPI 3.0% y/y (in line), Core 3.1% (in line), Services 4.4% (exp 4.3%)
BoE March cut probability rises to 95% from 84% pre-release
ING sticks with call for March cut and another by June
